mnemonics.title: ECDSA Signature Process
mnemonics.content: K-G-R-H-S: Know random k → Generate point → compute r → Hash message → compute s

mnemonics.title: Never Reuse k
mnemonics.content: Never Reuse Your Key's Nonce - If you repeat k, attackers can derive your private key easily

mnemonics.title: Public Key from Private Key
mnemonics.content: Q = dA × G - The private key (dA) multiplies the Generator point (G) to give the Public Key (Q)

acronyms.usage: Used in Bitcoin and Ethereum for authenticating transactions
The mathematical problem ECDSA security is based on
Encoding format used for ECDSA signatures in Bitcoin
Bitcoin transaction type using ECDSA verification
Specifies chain ID in signature 'v' value to prevent replay attacks

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a curve y² = x³ + 7, draw a line through two points P and Q; it intersects the curve at a third point R'. Reflect R' across the x-axis to get R = P + Q. For point doubling, draw tangent at P and reflect the intersection.

Private key is a random number d. Public key Q is found by 'walking' the curve d times from the base point G: G → 2G → 3G → ... → dG = Q. Going backward from Q to find d is like finding how many steps were taken—impossible without trying.
Signature = (r, s). r is like an x-coordinate from a random point. s binds the message to r using the private key. Both r and s are needed together—one alone reveals nothing useful.

analogies.analogy: The private key is like a password that signs documents. Anyone with the password can sign, but you never show the password—you just prove you know it by creating a signature that only that password could produce.
The public key is like a username. You can share it freely, and people can verify that signatures came from you by checking them against your public 'username' without ever needing your password.
The nonce k in ECDSA is like a one-time pad in encryption—using it twice completely breaks the system. Each signature needs a fresh, random k value to remain secure.
If RSA is a heavy truck, ECDSA is a sports car—they both get you there (secure your transactions), but ECDSA is much more efficient, faster, and requires less fuel (bandwidth and computation).
